seniority.level: Senior Manager / Director (people manager, leading a team)
level_signals: Responsible for hiring, developing, and scaling a team
Expected to engage with most senior leaders at DoorDash
Comp range $170K-$250K base, consistent with Senior Manager / Director

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: 8+ years of relevant management experience, ideally within a Marketplace or Subscription-focused business and/or founder experience
Track record of impact – you've been in the trenches and have a proven track record of driving significant, company-wide impact.
Bias to action and agility – you move quickly and thrive in a fast-paced environment. No job is beneath you and you lead from the front.
"Breakthrough walls" mentality – you are constantly pushing the envelope of what is possible and can figure out a way to make almost anything happen. You're a playmaker.
Clarity of thought – you can articulate a clear vision to cross-functional partners.
Skilled executive management and communication, especially at managing up and across DoorDash and going toe to toe with our most senior leaders.
Strong leadership and maturity to build (attract, hire, retain) and scale a world-class organization.
Proven ability to create clarity and focus in complex and ambiguous problem spaces; prioritization and pragmatism.
Demonstrated experience in building the team necessary to fulfill the strategy. Proactively involved in recruiting, mentoring, and developing team members.

nice_to_haves.raw_text_from_jd: 8+ years of relevant management experience, ideally within a Marketplace or Subscription-focused business and/or founder experience

scope.surface: DashPass — DoorDash's global subscription program within the Marketplace team
core_responsibilities: Drive DashPass' definition, strategy, and long-term vision
Manage, develop, and hire a team
Communicate strategy and results to all audiences including management team

green_flags: Compensation range disclosed ($170K-$250K base) with equity on top
Clear ownership of a high-priority business unit (DashPass)
Explicit people management scope — role has meaningful organizational authority
DoorDash is a scaled, public company with a recognizable consumer product
red_flags: Location type is not specified in the JD body — remote eligibility and office expectations are unclear
Visa sponsorship status not mentioned anywhere in the JD
Several requirements are vague cultural/behavioral attributes ('breakthrough walls mentality', 'clarity of thought') with no concrete operationalization, making it hard to self-assess fit
Wide comp band ($80K spread) suggests significant variance based on seniority tier or location, but the JD does not clarify which level within 'Senior Manager' this targets

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from comp range + 8+ years management + people management signals)
location.type set to 'unclear' — no explicit location or remote policy stated in JD body
ambiguities: JD mentions 'Remote Jobs Associated With Office in NYC' in the legal notice, suggesting some remote eligibility may exist, but the role location is not explicitly stated as remote, hybrid, or onsite anywhere in the JD body.

The 8+ years requirement says 'ideally within a Marketplace or Subscription-focused business and/or founder experience' — the word 'ideally' makes marketplace/subscription experience a nice-to-have, but the 8+ years management experience itself is treated as a hard requirement. Both interpretations are captured.
